item.title: This is exactly why we need to be teaching boys that yes, they can get sexually assaulted too, that consent goes both ways!! If it shouldn't be done to a girl then it done to a bloke!!! Consent goes both ways!
item.description: I don't know how old this post is, but it's so important men to know that they can talk about these things, that yes you get sexually assaulted too! If you know it shouldn't happen to a woman, then it shouldn't happen to you either!! I really hope that guy is ok and doing well now!

i am so happy to see this guy doing/looking well. and this is wholesome af. greetings friends
i just don't get why the table outline seems unfinished.. artistic choice?
good times
i'm sorry what
the regular pizza part looks good to me
looks like actor James Spader
minecwaft
aren't we all
mmm hmm
wasn't that something?
why is his mouth red
do it
Danny is 4 ft 10 in
man that burder looks good. guac and bacon? hell yeah
always used to wish i could do that. still do i guess.
tough to argue with this
not a deal breaker for me, unless she wanted me to physically transform into Randy or something like that. heck, even then probably
that's my set.. have a great night gang!!

item.dc:creator: 1AmadeusExMachina

item.title: I am no longer in agony, so FU to my American dentists.
item.description: I get nothing from endorsing anyone, but want this to be a PSA for others who may be suffering! (Or financially unable to get the care they need.) Summary: Begged for help for several months, in such pain I was getting suicidal, but problem solved in two days when went just a few steps past the border wall. 8 months of increasing pain, getting bounced from one dentist/specialist to another. Finally got one root canal for almost $2K, but they left a hole in the tooth and it was badly infected. "We can see you in 5 weeks". Meanwhile two other areas causing pain and waited weeks for each evaluation, paying up to $250 per appointment, and being told that next available treatment appointment is 7-10 weeks from now. (No insurance or financial restrictions - I was willing to max out the credit cards if needed!) I was in such pain that I couldn't even function normally, and was having suicidal thoughts for the first time in my long life. Then I heard about "Molar City": Los Algodones, Mexico. With 600 dentists there, I had no clue where to start, but a FB acquaintance told me about her dentists there, and within hours they were on a conference call with me and looking at my most recent x-rays. With a rough idea of what might be needed, I booked a flight from Tampa to Phoenix and drove to the border, crossing on foot. I'm an old woman, and my family and friends were nervous about this, but I was desperate, and willing to do anything. Turns out, this was the BEST thing! Never have I had such amazing medical CARE of any type. Two dentists at "Four Dental" examined my teeth, shuttled me to an Endodontist for a review of one tooth, and brought in a Periodontist for two others. The 4 of them collaborated on the safest plan of action, and offered me options. In TWO DAYS, I had 3 extractions, 1 implant, 2 bone grafts and two root canals - all pain free and for $2540. Light pain meds, anti-inflammatory med, and heavy duty antibiotics were prescribed, and they walked with me to the pharmacy to get them...$65. I never had ANY pain following the treatments. I booked the trip to stay an extra 3 days, thinking I would need time to recover, but it just turned into R&R in a very safe little town. They were checking on me as late as 8 PM, offering to bring food to my hotel, drive me back to the border, whatever I needed. Unbelievable! Amenities aside, they proved that this CAN be done, for a reasonable price, and without weeks of waiting. Clearly, their American counterparts just don't want to. Thank you Dr. Melony, Dr. David and Atonia! I'll be forever grateful.
item.dc:creator: nitaroyal

item.title: "Mooned"
item.dc:creator: BiNorwayBoy

item.title: A portrait I drew for my mom
item.description:
The portrait I drew for my mom. I haven't seen her in a while and not sure when I'm gonna see her again, if ever. I miss her terribly and for her birthday I made this portrait, it's a digital art made in Photoshop but my sister was able to print it on canvas and frame it as an actual painting. Doesn't look as good as oils but not bad either! Sadly I don't have a decent picture of the final result on hands.
This is my main reference.
And this is how she preferred to wear her hair. She looked terrific as a blondie with those long waves. I'm new here and honestly I have no idea how to make a proper post yet. Been a lurker for a long time and the community in here seems friendly, so I decided to join! I'm a digital artist (I do traditional drawing, too, but mainly I prefer to stick to my Photoshop and tablet), is it okay to share works that I do?
item.dc:creator: OddAmbrette

item.title: lmao, captain "free speech" is at it again...
item.description: Tweet link : https://x.com/simbasad2/status/1776957819788402938 For context - Elon claims to believe in "free speech", but has instructed his social media site to limit visibility of the prefix "cis" because it's allegedly a slur. Not the N-word. Not antisemitic shit. But "cis".

item.title: Phone clearing dump (mostly gaming memes)
item.description:
1. Imagine putting this on YouTube today
2
3. Which game actually has And You?
4. Those were the days
5. Wait is this background real?!
6
7
8. Bro is dead
9. Who wants a PS2?
10
11
12. Same
13. When my best friends ask me if I want to play games together
14.
15. Game devs: "Soon, I swear!" If you liked the humor of this dump I think you might like my wacky "Stanley Parable"-inspired game, called "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)"! Please wishlist, it would really encourage me to finish it: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Dont_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse_The_Game/ P. S. Have a great week and awesome and don't forget to hydrate and take care of yourself. Go on for a walk it helps with mental health! : )

item.title: I dump because I love you Dump
item.dc:creator: chicharrone

item.title: This is dumb and I don't know why I'm posting it.
item.description:
So, my partner and I were together for 11 years. It ended about as amicably as a relationship can end. I didn't want it to end but you need two to tango, right? Anyway, over the course of our relationship, she slowly got acclimated to proper camping and was getting pretty into it. And there were some personal things that made that kind of symbolic of some stuff she was working through. So when we split, I felt like it would be better for her to be able to keep that momentum going and have the equipment for it. And it was and she did keep using it. But it left me in a pickle, because I wasn't exactly in a position to just go and replace a full loadout of camping gear. I still had my backpack, so at least there was that. But I simply could not go out camping. Wasn't in the cards.
Today I picked up a new stove. I was going to just replace my Pocket Rocket 2 because I love that thing with a passion. But the local REI doesn't carry the pot I previously had, which I liked very much. And because in addition to being a fan of the outdoors, I'm also a big fucking weeb, and between Yuru Camp and various Japanese camping YouTube channels, my brain has been saturated with Soto stoves. And wouldn't you know it, REI had a combo kit featuring the Soto Amicus and a nice little pot with a lid that doubles as a pan/cup.
Pic I found in case anyone is wondering about the difference between the two. The one on the left is the one I now own. I still really like the folding stand design on the PR2 but the Amicus is definitely well-built. But I digress.
It's a really weird thing to get excited about because I can make coffee in my house. But as soon as I get the tent, I should be able to squeeze in a Spring camping trip before the sailing season starts up. And dang I'm excited. Again, no idea why I made this post except maybe I got a little giddy on caffeine. Please ignore me.
item.dc:creator: aThingWithTheStufAndTheJunk
